name: Source of the Larráun – running circular from Iribas (SL-NA 302)
name_original: Nacedero del Larráun – running circular desde Iribas (SL-NA 302)
region_intro: Jogging around Larraun offers diverse terrain within Navarre, Spain. The region is characterized by the scenic Plazaola Greenway, a former railway line providing relatively flat routes through an idyllic river valley. Beyond this, the Aralar mountains present varied trails for running, from challenging ascents to winding forest paths. This combination of river valleys and mountainous landscapes creates a refreshing environment for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Larraun The most popular jogging route is Source of the Larráun – running circular from Iribas (SL-NA 302), an easy 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes about 32 minutes to complete, exploring the source of the Larraun river. Another top favourite among local runners is Incredible views – Santa Cruz Tunnel loop from Uitzi, a difficult 15.9 miles (25.5 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views, including passage through a tunnel. Local runners also love the Cantina del Plazaola – Bartolo Tunnel loop from Lekunberri, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the Plazaola Greenway and its tunnels, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Jogging around Larraun is defined by the flat, scenic Plazaola Greenway, the challenging Aralar mountains, and picturesque river valleys. The network offers options for easy runs, moderate trails, and difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Larraun are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Larraun's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Larraun?
A: Larraun offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 80 routes available. These range from easy jogs to challenging trail runs through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Larraun?
A: You'll find diverse terrain in Larraun. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1118734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt; offers relatively flat, scenic paths along a former railway line, ideal for uninterrupted runs. For more challenging trail running, the Aralar mountains provide varied routes with ascents, winding forest paths, and open ridgelines.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or easy runs?
A: Yes, Larraun has options for all fitness levels. There are 2 easy running routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971608034/source-of-the-larraun-running-circular-from-iribas-sl-na-302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Larráun – running circular from Iribas (SL-NA 302)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a gentle 2.8-mile (4.5 km) trail exploring the source of the Larraun river.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Larraun?
A: The running routes in Larraun are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore the region's varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty and diverse options.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Larraun?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in Larraun are designed as circular paths, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971608034/source-of-the-larraun-running-circular-from-iribas-sl-na-302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Larráun – running circular from Iribas (SL-NA 302)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option.

Q: Are the running trails in Larraun dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in Larraun, especially those in natural areas like the Plazaola Greenway and the Aralar mountains, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971608034/source-of-the-larraun-running-circular-from-iribas-sl-na-302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Larráun – running circular from Iribas (SL-NA 302)&lt;/a&gt; are considered easy and suitable for families looking for a pleasant jog together. The relatively flat sections of the Plazaola Greenway also offer a good option for family outings.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the running paths?
A: Larraun is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning views in the Aralar mountains, with elevated points like Lakarri (1,046 m) and Belokain (968 m). Along some routes, you might encounter natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4107585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ixkier Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3049380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amezketa Stream Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leitzaran River Valley&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find any unique landmarks or historical sites while jogging?
A: Yes, Larraun offers several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1118734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt; itself is a historical railway line featuring unique unlit tunnels like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1750121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartolo Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4207308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel de Aralar Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, a 12th-century architectural jewel in the Aralar mountains, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3552575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aixita Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for running in Larraun?
A: Larraun's natural environment makes it appealing for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but higher altitude trails in the Aralar mountains provide cooler options. Winter running is possible, but some mountain paths may be affected by snow or ice, so checking local conditions is advisable.

Q: Are there running routes that pass through tunnels?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1118734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a former railway line, features several unlit tunnels, adding a unique element to your run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24645451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantina del Plazaola – Bartolo Tunnel loop from Lekunberri&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a route that includes passage through these tunnels.

Q: How can I access the trailheads using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Larraun's trailheads are available, particularly to towns like Lekunberri which serve as access points for the Plazaola Greenway and other routes. For specific bus routes and schedules to trailheads like those near the Plazaola Greenway or Nacedero del Larraun, it's best to consult local transport authority websites.

Q: Is parking available near the running routes?
A: Parking is generally available at key access points for popular running routes in Larraun, including near the Plazaola Greenway and trailheads for routes like the Source of the Larraun. Specific parking information can often be found on local tourism sites or detailed route descriptions.
tips: A beautiful natural place in a very picturesque setting with a variety of trails to get here. This place marks the source of the Iribas River which, downstream, joins the Ibaia River to become the well-known Larraun.
